commercialBoundary.title: Fenrua Labs Pty Ltd — Research and Technology Services
paragraphs: Fenrua Labs Pty Ltd researches, develops, and provides AI efficiency infrastructure software and related technology services through service subscriptions and client-specific business agreements. Its work may include software, infrastructure access, hosting, research, development, integration, technical support, and evidence-aware workflows within the scope of the relevant service or agreement.

Fenrua Labs Pty Ltd does not offer investments, token crowdfunding, securities, bonds, equity, debt, managed investment interests, profit-sharing arrangements, revenue-sharing arrangements, yield products, exchange products, trading products, or any financial-return scheme. Neither a subscription nor a client-specific business agreement gives, promises, expects, entitles, or represents profit, return, token appreciation, token allocation, liquidity, resale value, dividends, buyback rights, or ownership in Fenrua Labs Pty Ltd.

Fenrua Labs Pty Ltd does not operate a market, exchange, order book, trading venue, or public swap product. It does not provide financial, investment, legal, or tax advice, or recommend that a person buy, sell, hold, trade, or rely on an asset for financial gain.
serviceAgreementBoundary: Fenrua Labs Pty Ltd may enter into service subscriptions and client-specific business agreements, issue invoices, receive payment, and carry out the research, development, software, infrastructure, hosting, integration, support, and related technology activities covered by those arrangements.
Browsing this public website does not itself form a paid service agreement. The service description, price, scope, and terms presented or agreed for a particular service govern that service.
businessOperationsBoundary: Fenrua Labs Pty Ltd may receive payment for its lawful business activities through the commercial method agreed for the relevant service. The current public website is an information, evidence, and service-discovery surface and does not presently provide self-service account creation or checkout.

A payment settles the price of the relevant service. It does not by itself give a customer equity, ownership, profit-sharing, investment returns, token allocation, or rights outside the applicable service terms or client agreement.

offerings.description: Company information, service information, technical documentation, research, evidence records, status information, and bounded read-only observations.
Published within each artifact's stated revision, maturity, validation method, evidence, and limitations. Publication is not a universal certification or guarantee.
Bounded, signed, state-dependent public observations. They do not accept transactions or establish claims outside their stated evidence.

offerings.category: AI efficiency infrastructure software and related technology services
offerings.description: A service is offered with its own description, price, scope, and applicable terms.

offerings.category: Client-specific software, infrastructure access, hosting, integration, support, and related work
offerings.description: The relevant agreement, order, or statement of work defines the client-specific service.

offerings.category: Public self-service account, checkout, client upload, or interactive AI interface
offerings.status: NOT PART OF THE CURRENT PUBLIC SITE
offerings.description: These controls are not exposed by the current public release. This does not restrict separately offered or agreed services.

offerings.category: Investment, financial-return, exchange, trading, custody, or public-swap product
offerings.description: Outside the current service model and not created by a subscription, payment, agreement, or technical artifact.

technologyScope: Fenrua's business and technical direction includes research, development, software, infrastructure access, hosting, integration, technical support, evidence-aware workflows, operational efficiency, protocol design, and related technology services.

Identity, authority, integrity, policy, verification, containment, recovery, secret handling, and vulnerability reporting remain important technical functions where relevant. They are not the complete definition of Fenrua Labs' business or future direction.
requestHandling: The public observation endpoints derive a short-lived, per-instance salted abuse-control identifier from request network metadata. The application code does not retain or log the raw network address for that purpose.

Business email, private vulnerability reporting, and external provider logs are separate operational channels. Their public descriptions must reflect verified current handling rather than hypothetical future systems.

included: Public, point-in-time website artifacts generated from this repository.
The current public audit route and release manifest when generated for a release.
The public document register and non-live documentation listed there.
excluded: Dynamic live block-card data, endpoint responses, refresh state, signed observations, and all protected operational surfaces.
Signing keys, private gateways, validators, private meshes, protected infrastructure, Vercel secrets, and Vercel project internals.
Public standard-form business contracts, token materials, investment materials, trading features, and financial-return claims.

releaseEvidence.binding: A release manifest is generated from the exact release checkout. It binds only the listed public artifacts, their validation scope, and stated limitations; it does not attest to protected systems or live block-card data.
